title: goodenia psammophila
intro: Goodenia psammophila, a species of flowering plant in the family Goodeniaceae, is native to the eastern coastal regions of Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The leaves and stems of Goodenia psammophila are believed to have health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, due to their rich content of flavonoids and glycosides. Traditional herbal preparations of Goodenia psammophila include infusions and decoctions, often used to make teas and tonics for their medicinal properties.
